info.title: MCP Task Server
info.description: Model Context Protocol (MCP) server for AI-powered Task Management data injection. Exposes four tools that allow an AI agent to inspect the task schema, insert task records in bulk, retrieve aggregate statistics, and discover available capabilities.

get.summary: Return the JSON Schema for the Task resource
get.description: Returns a JSON Schema document that describes the shape of a single Task object as expected by the `POST /v1/mcp/tasks` insertion endpoint. AI agents use this schema to construct well-formed task payloads before calling the insertion tool.

200.description: JSON Schema for a Task input object

status.description: Current lifecycle status of the task

post.description: Accepts a JSON array of Task input objects and persists them to the database in a single transaction. Intended for AI-driven bulk data injection. All records in the request are inserted atomically; a validation failure in any item rejects the entire batch.

parameters.name: Idempotency-Key
parameters.description: Client-supplied UUID that makes the request idempotent. Repeated calls with the same key within 24 hours return the original response without re-inserting.

example.title: Design database schema
example.description: Define tables, columns, and indexes for the task management module.

get.summary: Return task count statistics grouped by status
get.description: Returns aggregate statistics for all task records currently in the database, broken down by status value. AI agents use this endpoint to validate that a previous bulk insertion produced the expected distribution of records.

get.summary: List available MCP tools and their descriptions
get.description: Returns a machine-readable description of every tool exposed by this MCP server, including the tool name, a natural-language description, the HTTP method and path, and a link to the relevant OpenAPI operation. Intended for AI agent discovery.

TaskStatus.description: Lifecycle status of a task. Set by the caller on creation; no server-side transition enforcement.

TaskInput.description: Payload for creating a single task. Server generates id, createdAt, and updatedAt.

description.description: Optional detailed description of the task.

dueDate.description: Optional deadline for the task (ISO 8601 date, e.g. 2026-12-31).

Task.description: A persisted task record as returned by the server.

id.description: Surrogate primary key, server-assigned on creation.

dueDate.description: Optional deadline for the task (ISO 8601 date, e.g. 2026-12-31).

TaskJsonSchema.description: A JSON Schema document (draft-07 compatible) that describes the shape of a TaskInput object. The exact content mirrors the TaskInput schema defined in this OpenAPI spec.

TaskSummary.description: Aggregate statistics for task records in the database.

counts.description: Number of tasks per status value.

total.description: Sum of all status counts; equals the total number of task records.

description.description: Natural-language description for AI agent tool selection.

path.description: HTTP path relative to the server base URL.

tools.name: mcp-schema-tasks
tools.description: Returns the JSON Schema describing the shape of a Task input object. Call this first to understand what fields are required before inserting tasks.

tools.name: mcp-tasks
tools.description: Accepts a JSON array of Task objects and persists them to the database. Use the schema from mcp-schema-tasks to construct valid payloads.

tools.name: mcp-tasks-summary
tools.description: Returns task counts grouped by status (TODO, IN_PROGRESS, DONE) and total. Use this to validate that bulk insertion produced the expected data distribution.

tools.name: mcp-help
tools.description: Returns this list of available MCP tools and their descriptions.

field.description: JSON path to the offending field, e.g. '[2].status'.

reason.description: Human-readable explanation of the validation failure.

code.description: Machine-readable error code, e.g. INVALID_INPUT, VALIDATION_FAILED.

details.description: Per-field validation errors, present on 400 and 422 responses.

BadRequest.description: Malformed request body or missing required fields

error.message: Request body is not valid JSON or is missing required fields.

UnprocessableEntity.description: Request is syntactically valid but semantically rejected (e.g. unknown status value)

error.message: One or more task records contain invalid field values.

details.reason: must be one of TODO, IN_PROGRESS, DONE

error.message: An unexpected error occurred. Please try again later.

tags.name: MCP Tools
tags.description: Endpoints exposed as MCP tools for AI agent interaction. See ADR-002 for transport details and ADR-003 for tool registration strategy.
